title: Morning Robotics Scan
report:
## US Army Uses Drones to Clear Breach

### The Breakthrough
The US Army used 35 drones and 100 pounds of C4 explosives to clear a breach, demonstrating the potential of uncrewed systems in reducing soldier risks. This exercise involved 25 attack drones targeting bunkers and obstacles, while other drones dropped smoke canisters to obscure the battlefield. Two uncrewed ground vehicles packed with C4 explosives destroyed remaining obstacles, demonstrating the potential of autonomous systems in combat.

### The Caveats
The effectiveness of this approach in varying combat scenarios and environments remains untested.

## Continual Robot Policy Learning via Variational Neural Dynamics

### The Breakthrough
A new framework enables robots to learn from experience and improve task performance under changing dynamics. This approach combines an analytical physics prior with a neural residual for unmodeled effects and uses a recurrent encoder to infer the current hidden condition from recent interaction. The method has been tested in simulation and on a real quadrotor, showing improved performance under diverse unobserved disturbances.

### The Caveats
The approach, while demonstrated on a specific quadrotor task, lacks comparisons to named baselines.

## RouterVLA: Supervision for VLA Selection

### The Breakthrough
RouterVLA uses outcome-disjoint cross-fitting to build profiles for each frozen expert and score the selected expert without entering its profile. The method achieved a 14.64pp gain in held-out success across 34,752 rollout records. The study also found that learned scorers are statistically indistinguishable from a transparent probe-success rule.

### The Caveats
The absence of stated baselines means it is unclear whether RouterVLA's gain in success rates surpasses existing methods.

## World Action Models for Continual Imitation

### The Breakthrough
The Recurrent Generative Replay (REGEN) framework uses World Action Models to generate pseudo-replay trajectories, allowing robots to rehearse previously learned tasks. Experiments demonstrate a reduction in catastrophic forgetting by up to 50% compared to sequential fine-tuning.

### The Caveats
While the proposed REGEN framework shows promise, its comparison to only sequential fine-tuning and privileged experience replay methods leaves unclear how it would perform against other state-of-the-art continual imitation learning approaches.

## Scalable Behavior Cloning

### The Breakthrough
ABC includes ABC-130K, the largest open-source teleoperation dataset with 130K episodes across 195 diverse tasks, and provides a co-training recipe for reliable simulation and real-world evaluation. The researchers explore various training recipes and compare architectural choices for Diffusion Transformers and Vision-Language-Action models, achieving successful execution of dexterous tasks.

### The Caveats
Without comparison to existing behavior cloning methods or baselines, it is unclear how the proposed ABC stack and its components perform relative to state-of-the-art approaches.

## OpenAgentd: Self-Hosted AI Agent OS

### The Breakthrough
OpenAgentd combines a desktop application with a web-based cockpit, allowing users to interact with their AI agents in a more visual and interactive way. The system includes features like a command palette, slash commands, and an inspector that shows every tool call and result. It also supports multi-agent teams, where a lead agent can spawn specialist instances on demand and coordinate with them through an async mailbox.

### The Caveats
Without any formal evaluation or comparison to existing AI agent operating systems, it remains unclear how OpenAgentd's performance and capabilities stack up against other solutions.

## oomwoo: Open-Source Robot Vacuum

### The Breakthrough
The oomwoo project combines 3D printing, Raspberry Pi, and ROS2 to create an affordable and customizable robot vacuum. It uses 2D LiDAR for mapping and navigation, and it is designed to be locally controlled with native Home Assistant integration. The project is organized into modules, allowing multiple developers to contribute to different parts of the project in parallel.

### The Caveats
The project's claims of affordability and effectiveness have not been tested or compared against existing commercial robot vacuum cleaners.

## Mirage: Unified Virtual File System for AI Agents

### The Breakthrough
Mirage mounts services and data sources as a single filesystem, enabling AI agents to read, write, and execute commands across different backends using standard bash commands. The system provides a unified interface for accessing various services, eliminating the need for multiple SDKs and APIs. It also supports portable workspaces, allowing agents to move between machines without restarting or reconfiguring the system.

### The Caveats
The unified interface and support for 50 backends are demonstrated through examples and documentation, but without formal evaluation or comparison to existing solutions, it is unclear how Mirage's performance and scalability stack up against established alternatives.

## Finding It Challenging to Maintain Software Created with Coding Agents?

### The Breakthrough
The use of coding agents has increased productivity, but also introduced new challenges in maintaining the resulting code base. The writer mentions the importance of having proper PRDs, system designs, and implementation plans in place, but notes that these are not sufficient to overcome the difficulties of working with AI-generated code.

### The Caveats
The challenge of maintaining AI-generated code is presented as a personal anecdote without a systematic comparison of engineering processes or code review methods.

## role-model: Hybrid Local/Cloud AI Router

### The Breakthrough
role-model uses a deterministic approach with fallback to a controller model and assigns domains and roles to models. It also supports routing between multiple local models via vendored llama-swap and allows for the evaluation of model performance on real tasks. The system can assess request difficulty and make decisions based on user-defined criteria.

### The Caveats
The effectiveness of role-model in real-world scenarios is unclear, as there are no stated comparisons to existing routing protocols or baseline methods for hybrid local/cloud AI.

### Priority
The US Army's use of drones to clear a breach is the lead story today due to its direct application of robotics and autonomous systems in a real-world scenario, demonstrating significant technical breakthroughs in the field.

### One action
Explore the US Army's breach-clearing exercise as a potential model for reducing risks in high-stakes missions by leveraging uncrewed systems and autonomous technologies.
